content.text: Let's build the precompact hook feature. We need to intercept /compact before it fires.

I'll implement the PreCompact hook. The key design decisions are: (1) exit code 2 blocks compaction, (2) we use claude -p for quality summaries, (3) token estimation via chars/4 since JSONL is inflated by hook attachments.

Let me start by creating the hook file.
good, make sure you handle the case where claude -p hangs after printing

Right — claude -p does post-response cleanup after printing, which causes spawnSync to hang for the full timeout. I'll use async spawn with a kill-after-close handler instead. The stdout arrives before the hang, so we capture it and kill the process when the close event fires.

This avoids burning 45 seconds on every invocation.
